title: Rural and Northern Immigration Pilot applicant needs a work permit - Canada.ca
content:
## You need a work permit Rural and Northern Immigration Pilot applicant needs a work permit
If you’re applying to the Rural and Northern Immigration Pilot (RNIP) , you need a work permit to work in Canada.
Based on your answer, you may be eligible for a 1-year employer-specific work permit (opens in a new tab) . You must also meet the general eligibility requirements (opens in a new tab) for a work permit.

## Work permits for spouses or common-law partners
Your spouse or common-law partner can apply for an open work permit (opens in a new tab) at the same time as you apply for your 1-year work permit.
Their work permit will only let them work in the same community as you.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
